# observe_monitor_v2_mute_rule



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the mute rule. This should clearly indicate the purpose of the mute
(e.g., "Weekly Maintenance Window", "Holiday Freeze Period").
- `schedule` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Defines when this mute rule is active. Must specify exactly one schedule type: one_time for a single time window, or recurring for cron-based repeating windows. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--schedule))
- `workspace` (String) OID of the workspace this object is contained in.

### Optional

- `criteria` (Block List, Max: 1) Optional conditional expression to selectively mute notifications based on alert properties. If omitted, all notifications from the target monitor(s) are muted. Global mute rules (no monitor_id) MUST specify criteria to prevent accidentally muting all alerts in the workspace. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--criteria))
- `description` (String) Optional detailed description explaining why this mute rule exists, what it covers,
and any relevant context for operators.
- `folder` (String) OID of the folder containing this mute rule. Mute rules inherit folder-based
permissions, allowing teams to organize and control access to their mute rules.
- `icon_url` (String) Optional icon URL to visually identify this mute rule in the Observe UI.
- `managed_by` (String) Optional OID of the app or system managing this mute rule. When set, the mute rule
is considered "managed" and may have restricted manual editing in the UI.
- `monitor_id` (String) Optional OID of the specific monitor this mute rule applies to. If omitted, the
mute rule is "global" and applies to all monitors in the workspace (subject to
criteria filtering). Global mute rules must specify criteria.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `is_conditional` (Boolean) Computed read-only boolean indicating whether this mute rule has criteria (true) or
applies unconditionally to all matching notifications (false).
- `is_global` (Boolean) Computed read-only boolean indicating whether this is a global mute rule (true) or
monitor-specific (false). Global mutes apply to all monitors subject to criteria.
- `oid` (String) OID (Observe ID) for this object. This is the canonical identifier that
should be used when referring to this object in terraform manifests.
- `valid_from` (String) Computed read-only field indicating when the mute rule is currently active from.
For one-time mutes, this is the start_time. For recurring mutes, this is the start
of the current active window (may be in the past if still within the duration).
- `valid_to` (String) Computed read-only field indicating when the current mute window expires.
For one-time mutes with end_time, this is the end_time. For recurring mutes, this
is valid_from + duration. Null if the mute has no expiration (one-time without end_time).

<a id="nestedblock--schedule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule`

Required:

- `type` (String) Schedule type: "OneTime" for a single time window, or "Recurring" for cron-based repeating windows.

Optional:

- `one_time` (Block List, Max: 1) One-time mute schedule configuration. Use this for planned events like deployments, migrations, or known outages with a specific start and end time. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--schedule--one_time))
- `recurring` (Block List, Max: 1) Recurring mute schedule configuration. Use this for regular maintenance windows (e.g., weekly deployments, nightly batch jobs, weekend maintenance). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--schedule--recurring))

<a id="nestedblock--schedule--one_time"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule.one_time`

Required:

- `start_time` (String) Start time for the mute window in RFC3339 format (e.g., "2025-12-17T10:00:00Z"). The mute becomes active at this time.

Optional:

- `end_time` (String) Optional end time for the mute window in RFC3339 format. If omitted, the mute remains active indefinitely until manually deleted. For safety, it's recommended to always specify an end time.


<a id="nestedblock--schedule--recurring"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule.recurring`

Required:

- `cron_schedule` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Cron schedule defining when each mute window starts. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--schedule--recurring--cron_schedule))
- `duration` (String) How long each mute window lasts, specified as a Go duration string. Examples: "1h" (1 hour), "30m" (30 minutes), "4h30m" (4.5 hours). The mute starts at the cron-scheduled time and remains active for this duration.

<a id="nestedblock--schedule--recurring--cron_schedule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `schedule.recurring.cron_schedule`

Required:

- `timezone` (String) IANA timezone name for interpreting the cron schedule (e.g., "America/New_York", "Europe/London", "UTC"). Required for recurring schedules to ensure consistent behavior across daylight saving time transitions.

Optional:

- `raw_cron` (String) Standard cron expression (5 fields: minute hour day month weekday). Examples: "0 2 * * 0" = Every Sunday at 2:00 AM, "0 22 * * 1-5" = Weekdays at 10:00 PM, "0 0 1 * *" = First day of each month at midnight. If omitted, the mute evaluates continuously (not recommended for recurring mutes).




<a id="nestedblock--criteria"></a>
### Nested Schema for `criteria`

Required:

- `compare_terms` (Block List, Min: 1) List of comparison conditions to evaluate against alert properties. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--criteria--compare_terms))
- `operator` (String) Boolean operator combining comparison terms: "And" (all must match) or "Or" (any must match).

<a id="nestedblock--criteria--compare_terms"></a>
### Nested Schema for `criteria.compare_terms`

Required:

- `column` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Name of the alert property column to compare (e.g., "severity", "host", "environment"). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--criteria--compare_terms--column))
- `comparison` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) Comparison operation to perform. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--criteria--compare_terms--comparison))

<a id="nestedblock--criteria--compare_terms--column"></a>
### Nested Schema for `criteria.compare_terms.column`

Optional:

- `column_path` (Block List, Max: 1) Specifies how the user wants to group by a specific column name or a JSON object column that has a path. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--criteria--compare_terms--column--column_path))
- `link_column` (Block List, Max: 1) Identifies a link-type column created by connecting two different datasets' columns (primary sources & destination sources). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--criteria--compare_terms--column--link_column))

<a id="nestedblock--criteria--compare_terms--column--column_path"></a>
### Nested Schema for `criteria.compare_terms.column.column_path`

Required:

- `name` (String) The name of the column.

Optional:

- `path` (String) The path of the path, if the name refers to a column with a JSON object.


<a id="nestedblock--criteria--compare_terms--column--link_column"></a>
### Nested Schema for `criteria.compare_terms.column.link_column`

Required:

- `name` (String) The name of the link column.



<a id="nestedblock--criteria--compare_terms--comparison"></a>
### Nested Schema for `criteria.compare_terms.comparison`

Required:

- `compare_fn` (String) the type of comparison (greater, less, equal, etc.)

Optional:

- `value_bool` (List of Boolean) list of size <=1 consisting of a boolean value.
- `value_duration` (List of String) list of size <=1 consisting of a duration value.
- `value_float64` (List of Number) list of size <=1 consisting of a float value.
- `value_int64` (List of Number) list of size <=1 consisting of an integer value.
- `value_string` (List of String) list of size <=1 consisting of a string value.
- `value_timestamp` (List of String) list of size <=1 consisting of a timestamp value.
